title: #6 - Elias: The Man on the Mountain

blocks.text: Hey everyone;

I finally caught up with these posts. I am now writing about yesterday rather than several days in the past. Yesterday was another remarkable day. We have a running joke that I keep saying "This is the most incredible thing I have ever seen" almost every single day.
Guess what?
Yesterday I said it again 😂

blocks.caption: To the mountain man, and to the hot springs again.

blocks.text: This was our third day in Methena, we are still here and I think we will be staying till Sunday. We will enjoy our last day here and maybe finally climb up to the summit of one of these volcanos. We have only ever gotten near the summit thus far and it would be great to be able to go to the very top.

We woke up yet again and took it easy all morning. Just passing the hours writing, reading, thinking, and eating. We all spent quite a bit of time asking Alex about the Greek language and practicing phrases we can use in the shops when we go to buy things.
We also spent the day before heading out backing delicious Italian bread from scratch.
Making the bread took a while, and I cannot take credit for the bread in the slightest except that I helped carry the ingredients up the mountain from the grocery store to the villa 😂 .
We did not eat the bread right after, and I will explain why in a minute.

Remember yesterday when we were biking back to the villa from groceries and we met Elias, the man who made the hot spring useable, and was driving the truck? Well, during this conversation he invited us to his home to have a BBQ.
So we decided we would bring the bread we made, and a 3 euro bottle of wine up to his home for the BBQ.
We left to go to his home just before sunset. We wanted to be there around 7 pm

We took the beautiful roads and climbed even higher to get to his home. Elias lives in a village of 9 people about 350 metres up the volcano. So we biked with the bread and wine up to his home for the BBQ.
We arrived to the village and I Kai and I practiced our Greek asking if they where where Elias lives.

We tried to say "Pu ine ho Elias, parikalo?" Which means Where is Elias, please? And what actually came out what "Pu ine ho Elios, parikalo" which actually means "Where is the sun" haha. Coincidentally the sun and Elias's home happened to be in the exact same place. So we ended up finding his home.
Elias's home was the home at the very highest point of the village. We all had to push up our bikes to make it to his home

blocks.caption: Pushing up bikes to his house

blocks.text: Listening to Elias speak to us about his home felt sort of like watching a show the the History TV channel. As it turns out Elias and Methena starred in a famous Greek Documentry. There is a lot of forgotten history here.
Elias was excited to share everything with us. There was a lot to see and explain. His home was filled with unique history that unlike anything I have seen before.
Here are some of the things he mentioned, I dont think I can get them all.

blocks.caption: 200 year old home - the last original home on Methena.

items: He lived in a home that was 200 years old and the last home on Methena in the traditional style. All the other homes on Methena in this style has become ruins and rebuilt in other villages. No one else lived in these styles of homes still.

He and the house is fully self sufficient. He has a thrasher (something to ground wheat or barley?), barley, olive trees, fruit farms, chicken coup, wood working shop, makes his own tsipuro (Greek hard liquor, definitely spelt wrong), he collects his own rain water. He stores bout 50 tons of water. Methena has no natural water and uses 100% rain water.
He restored his ancient home, and is building a new home at the same time. He is self taught through Youtube (a Canadian youtube channel 😼 ).
So many cats, All the village cats come to his home.
Incredible view of the village, valley, and ocean below. Breathtaking spot and he had a great patio to sit and have a beer.

Elias is a man happy with the place he found for himself. He felt as thought it was fate that brought him to find this house and that the house was made for him. He found out afterwards that the previous owner of the house died the very same month that he was born. A new beginning?

He knows everything about the island. The locals, the tradition. Sad about the fact that the island and its culture is dying. He is 40 years old and he is the youngest person living on the island. In some years there will be no locals left.
He has a beautiful wood shop with collectors tools all these Stanly brand planes. I dont know what those are but he was really excited about them.

George Batis - a famous Greek musician was born in his house. This was in the 30s and all the village back then would come gather in his house for concerts. Back then the village has around 300-400 people and was much bigger than the current nine people who lived there.

Elias is an brilliant photographer and showed us a photo book with all the history of the village. He had photos of all the elder locals doing traditional town tasks such as plowing fields with mules, or washing all the village clothes in a watching. I'll add one of the photos below.

blocks.text: Elias made us a delicious all you can eat BBQ. We ate pork, Greek salad with fresh feta made from the village goats and tomates picked from his very garden. We drank his tsiporo made by him and our 3 euro wine and talked about old island stories.

After listening and learning about Methena from Elias we all felt like we learned something special about Methana. We were all left a little sad about the fact that the islands original culture is slowly being lost.
After the BBQ we biked down the mountain to go to the hot spring. We enjoyed the hot spring until late in the night.

blocks.caption: Adam happy as can be harvesting Barley

blocks.text: Adam was really excited to chop up some Barley and held Elias with his harvest. I got to give it a try as well. He told us that this weekend we will use the Barley to make beer.

blocks.caption: turn the phone sideways to see :)

blocks.text: This was one of the photos I loved. This is one of the ladies in the village using the last donkey on Methena to carry sticks. She carries a really lovely smile in the photo. According to Elias, many of the locals on Methena NEVER have left the island, some have never even left their small villages.

blocks.caption: The view from Elias mountain village home

blocks.text: This was the view from the balcony on his house that is under construction. Elias lives in a remarkable place with a breathtaking view.
He loved the home we was building. We were all so shocked that he learned how to build his home on YouTube.

I only got probably about half written down here of that Elias had in his house and shared with us. He had many more historical stories about his home such as showing us where the town mayor used to make his wine, or where the villagers washed their clothes on the washing stones.
That's all for today.
I slept in the same villa bed as before. And here is a picture of all of Elias's cats if you are curious.
See you tomorrow.
-Erik
